name: Cranberry-Orange Shrub
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium saucepan, bring 1 cup of water to a boil. Add the cranberries and reduce heat. Simmer until soft, about 10 minutes.
2. Remove from heat and pass through a food mill or sieve; keep warm.
3. In a large bowl, combine remaining cold water with gelatin. Let soften for 3-5 minutes.
4. Stir in hot cranberries and honey to dissolve gelatin evenly.
5. Cool mixture to room temperature. Add orange juice and stir well.
6. Cover and refrigerate until chilled (4-8 hours).
7. Beat milk into the mixture, pour into a shallow metal dish, and freeze until solid.
8. Break into chunks and process in a food processor or puree with a hand mixer.
9. Beat egg white until stiff and fold into slush.
10. Return to freezing pan and freeze at least 30 more minutes. If frozen solid, transfer to refrigerator for 15 minutes before serving.
11. Serve chilled in goblets (4 servings, approximately 170 calories each).
